changes_applied.fix: Reframe description with 'when' context and concrete trigger phrases
changes_applied.details: New description leads with 'Use whenever someone is about to write a mock'. Lists explicit phrase triggers ('just mock it', 'jest.mock', 'unittest.mock', 'sinon', 'XCTest', 'staging is slow so I'll fake it'). Added 4 new YAML triggers.

changes_applied.fix: Add 'When to use' section above scope
changes_applied.details: Frames the skill around the moment of temptation (flaky API, slow dep, dev DB down) rather than passive 'this skill handles X'. Makes invocation more obvious.
After the calendar diagram, added a concrete scenario (API field renamed users->data, mocks pass, real staging crashes). Shows the cost of mocking in 2 sentences.

changes_applied.fix: Inline blocked code patterns in 4 languages
changes_applied.details: Added a 'Patterns that get blocked inline' subsection with code samples in JavaScript, Python, Swift, Go. Previously the catalog was only in references/. Inline examples make pattern recognition immediate without reading the reference.

changes_skipped.reason: The pre-tool-use hook already does runtime mock detection. A separate scan script would be valuable but is out of scope for in-session improvements (would require new file content + hook integration). Preflight skill could call it later.

description_before: Iron rule: no mocks/stubs/test files. Hook blocks *.test.ts, __tests__/, jest.mock(), etc. When mocking tempts: diagnose why real system unavailable, fix it, validate real system instead.
description_after: Use whenever someone is about to write a mock, stub, test double, or test file — or is already tempted to. This skill explains why mocking creates false confidence (mock drift), shows what gets blocked and what doesn't, and redirects to real-system validation via a concrete DIAGNOSE → FIX → VERIFY flow. Trigger on phrases like 'just mock it', 'stub out the API', 'add a test file', 'jest.mock', 'unittest.mock', 'sinon', 'XCTest', 'vitest', 'staging is slow so I'll fake it', or whenever the real system feels inconvenient.
self_review_notes: The growth (80->119) is mostly the inline code patterns and the real-world scenario — both are high-leverage because they let the model recognize violations without loading the reference file. Description now triggers on the language and frameworks that signal mocking intent, not just abstract concepts.
